name: ios_length_mismatch
code:
proof fn phi_1_ios_length_mismatch(packets: Seq<CPacket>)
    requires
        packets.len() > 0,
    ensures
        map_sent_packet_seq_to_ios(packets).len() != packets.len(),
{
}
reason: If ios length differs from packets length, deliver_packet_seq's bijection between sent packets and io events is broken

name: empty_packets_nonempty_ios
reason: Mapping an empty packet sequence should yield empty ios; non-empty output from nothing would fabricate send events

name: mapped_ios_not_all_sends
code:
proof fn phi_3_mapped_ios_not_all_sends(packets: Seq<CPacket>)
    requires
        packets.len() > 0,
    ensures
        !all_ios_are_sends(map_sent_packet_seq_to_ios(packets)),
{
}
reason: map_sent_packet_seq_to_ios wraps every packet in Send; if all_ios_are_sends were false, the ensures of deliver_packet_seq would be inconsistent

name: invalid_send_passes_check
reason: no_invalid_sends must reject any Send carrying InvalidMessage; passing here means the guard is vacuously true or mis-specified

name: singleton_packet_set_empty
code:
proof fn phi_5_singleton_packet_set_empty(cp: CPacket)
    requires
        cp.abstractable(),
    ensures
        abstractify_seq_of_cpackets_to_set_of_sht_packets(seq![cp]) == Set::<Packet>::empty(),
{
}
reason: Abstractifying a singleton packet sequence to an empty set would silently discard outbound packets, violating deliver_packet_seq's completeness guarantee
